zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth Caldwell於大約1665年出生在英國爾格拉本哈爾時，她的父親Richard Caldwell27歲，母親Rebecca Sharples36歲。她於1694年8月4日與William Thomas Swaffer在美國賓夕法尼亞州切斯特雷利 堂史普結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1694年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州切斯特雷利 堂史普。她去世於1760年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州費拉德爾福亞弗雷德里克 堂史普享年96歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Caldwell于大约1665年出生在英国爾格拉本哈爾时，她的父亲Richard Caldwell27岁，母亲Rebecca Sharples36岁。她于1694年8月4日与William Thomas Swaffer在美国宾夕法尼亚州切斯特雷利 堂史普结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她于1694年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州切斯特雷利 堂史普。她去世于1760年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州費拉德爾福亞弗雷德里克 堂史普享年96岁。

nameMeanings.name: Caldwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

variants.name: Caddell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Caldwell  .
Irish (Dublin, Galway): variant of   Cadle  .

variants.name: Cadle
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Irish, both of Norman origin: nickname from Old French cadel ‘leader’.
English: variant of   Caldwell   and of   Caudill  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Kadel  .

variants.name: Cardwell
meanings: English and Scottish: in northern England, the West Midlands, and southwest Scotland, this surname is a variant of Caldwell . In southwestern England it is probably a variant of Cardall, a habitational name from Cardwell in Milton Abbot (Devon). See also Cardell .

variants.name: Chadwell
meanings: English: variant of   Caldwell  .

variants.name: Cadwell
meanings: English:
(Lancashire): variant of   Caldwell  .
(Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one of two Devon places, either Cadewell (House) in Cockington or Caddywell in Great Torrington.

variants.name: Coldwell
meanings: English (northern): variant of   Caldwell  .

variants.name: Caudill
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English caudel, cadel, caudell(e) ‘hot fortified drink, esp. one containing spiced wine or ale’, also a thin gruel (Anglo-Norman French caudel, medieval Latin caldellum). Alternatively, a variant of Caldwell . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cordwell
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Caldwell . Alternatively, in Derbyshire a habitational name from Cordwell in Holmsfield, Derbyshire, named with Old English cauld ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1682: Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1685: Chester, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Chester, Pennsylvania, United States

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

historicEvents.title: Gregorian Calendar is Adopted
historicEvents.description: Gregorian calendar was adopted in England in 1752. That year, Wednesday, September 2, 1752, was followed by Thursday, September 14th, 1752, which caused the country to skip ahead eleven days.

historicEvents.title: Seven Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Seven Years' War began as a North American conflict then stretched between England and France. England, along with allies, battled France in America, India, and Europe, making it arguably the first global war. The conflict ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763 and England was victorious. The Seven Years' war ultimately led to discontent in the colonies and the American Revolution.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Caldwell was born about 1665, in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, her father, Richard Caldwell, was 27 and her mother, Rebecca Sharples, was 36. She married William Thomas Swaffer on 4 August 1694, in Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. She lived in Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America in 1694. She died in 1760, in Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Elizabeth Caldwell est née environ 1665, à Grappenhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Caldwell, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Rebecca Sharples, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé William Thomas Swaffer le 4 août 1694, à Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Elle a vécu à Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1694. Elle est décédée en 1760, à Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Elizabeth Caldwell um 1665 in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Caldwell 27 und ihre Mutter Rebecca Sharples 36. Sie hat am 4. August 1694 in Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten William Thomas Swaffer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1694 hat sie in Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America gelebt. Sie starb 1760 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Caldwell è nata il circa 1665 nel Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Richard Caldwell, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Rebecca Sharples, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato William Thomas Swaffer il 4 agosto 1694, nel Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America nel 1694. Morì nel 1760, nel Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, all'età di 96 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Caldwellが約1665年にイギリスチェシュアイアグラップエンハルで生まれた時，父Richard Caldwellは27歳，母Rebecca Sharplesは36歳であった。1694年8月4日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州チェスターリドリー トウンシュイップでWilliam Thomas Swafferと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1694年にはイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州チェスターリドリー トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼女は1760年にイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州フィラデルフィアフレデリック トウンシュイップで96歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Caldwell(이)가 대략 1665년 영국 체실르 그랩홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Caldwell(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rebecca Sharples(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1694년 8월 4일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 체스터 리들리 타운시프에서 William Thomas Swaffer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1694년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 체스터 리들리 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1760년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 필라들필아 프레더릭크 타운시프에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Caldwell nasceu aproximadamente 1665, em Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Caldwell, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Rebecca Sharples, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com William Thomas Swaffer em 4 de agosto de 1694, em Ridley Township, Chester, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela viveu em Ridley Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America em 1694. Ela faleceu em 1760, em Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, com 96 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Caldwell родилась около 1665 г. в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Caldwell было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Rebecca Sharples, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Thomas Swaffer 4 августа 1694 г. в местности ридли тоуншип, честир, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она жила в местности ридли тоуншип, честир, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1694 году. Она умерла в 1760 г. в местности фредирикк тоуншип, филаделфя, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 96 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Elizabeth Caldwell nació aproximadamente 1665, en Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Caldwell, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Rebecca Sharples, tenía 36 años. Se casó con William Thomas Swaffer el 4 de agosto de 1694, en Ridley Township, Chester, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en Ridley Township, Chester, Pensilvania, British Colonial America en 1694. Murió en 1760, en Frederick Township, Philadelphia, Pensilvania, British Colonial America, a la edad de 96 años.
